ops.title: In cc-notes, `gh run list --limit 1` grabs the wrong run — pushes trigger CI, Reconcile, and cc-notes workflows; watch by workflow+commit and check job conclusions

ops.body:
Every push to cc-notes main triggers three workflows (CI, Reconcile, cc-notes). Watching CI via `gh run list --limit 1` latched onto Reconcile twice and produced a false "CI fully green" report — the viz landing's 14 lint failures shipped unnoticed because of it.

**Why:** `--limit 1` returns whichever workflow run was created last; `gh run watch --exit-status` then exits 0 on that unrelated run. A green watch is not a green CI.

**How to apply:** Resolve the run ID first with `gh run list --workflow CI --commit <sha> --json databaseId`, watch that ID, then list per-job conclusions (`gh api .../runs/$RID/jobs`) instead of trusting the watch exit code alone. `--commit` needs the FULL 40-char sha — a short sha silently returns an empty list (observed 2026-07-13: a poll loop keyed on a 12-char sha spun blind for 10+ minutes while the run had long finished; `git rev-parse` it first). Related: [[jj-commit-snapshots-whole-worktree]] — the same close-out discipline applies when the user has concurrent WIP in the tree.
